overview: I built 7 tools across 3 domains and 4 agents in ~1,500 lines of Python, progressing from a calculator to an autonomous research agent. Key lessons: tool descriptions are prompt engineering, context fills fast (10,000+ tokens by call 5), and autonomy isn't free.

keyPoints.title: Tool descriptions control Claude's behavior, not just usage
Tool use costs 2-3x more API calls than basic conversation
Research agent: only 4 successful fetches out of 20 attempts
80% of production code is resilience, 20% is functionality
Tool-chaining replaced workflow logic with runtime reasoning
